entry.title: Re: Hide Award Configuration Fields from Workflow Approval Page of Reward and Recognition
entry.content:
@deepaksajjan While I wait for feedback, can you test this ?

2. "Respect Permission" on the Workflow Can Restrict Field Visibility If the workflow is configured with Respect Permission = Yes, participants on the workflow (Approver, Contributor, CC Role steps) will only see fields they have RBP permissions for. This means you could configure the approver's RBP role to have No Access on specific fields, effectively hiding them from that role on the approval page. [2] By default, Respect Permission = No, meaning all workflow participants see all fields regardless of RBP. Once set to Yes, field-level RBP overrides are respected on the workflow details page. Important caveat: This setting does not retroactively affect already-triggered workflows — it only applies to newly created workflow instances after the change. [2]

entry.title: Unable to assign new GL account to custom movement type which is a copy of existing movement type
entry.content:
We have a business scenario where separate consumption account need to be determined if the material is issued to cost center for R&D purposes and regular production consumption. 

We created a custom movement type 901 which is a copy of movement type 201 and assign a account modifier ZBR. Please see attached screenshot for additional information.

while setting up automatic account determination, we do not see general modification "ZBR" in the drop down. Please see screenshot for additional information. 

We cannot create a separate valuation class as it means we have to create a separate material for the same material which is practically infeasible suggestion.

Is there any enhancement option available to change the account assignment or is this item on the roadmap?

entry.title: Re: Error in Manage Automatic Payments app
entry.content:
Dear @Zlizardo,

No. In Public Cloud there is no standard function in Manage Automatic Payments / Payments Processed to manually add documents or attachments directly to the payment run. The app is intended to schedule payment proposals or payments and to monitor the proposal/payment status For a processed run you can display and print the proposal and payment lists and download the payment media but the app provides no option to store attachments on the run itself

More details below:

SAP Help – Manage Automatic Payments

SAP Help – Payment Proposal Lists and Payment Lists

As a clean-core workaround, supporting documents should be stored on the related standard business object, for example on the supplier invoice or on the related journal entry, where attachment handling is supported in the standard process. If a payment-run-level document repository is required, the clean-core option is a side-by-side document register or workflow on SAP BTP, linked by payment run ID, company code, fiscal year and/or accounting document number, while keeping the Cloud core unchanged

More details below:

SAP Help – Supplier Invoice Apps Grouped by Basic Operations

SAP Help – Manage Journal Entries – New Version

SAP Help – Document Management

Where the business requirement is to attach documents directly to the payment run in Manage Automatic Payments this is not covered by the current standard app behavior. The correct approach is to submit an improvement request in SAP Customer Influence.

More details below:

SAP KBA 2963059 – How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Summary:

The missing add document icon in Payments Processed is expected standard behavior. Attachments should be maintained on the related invoice or accounting document not directly on the payment run in Manage Automatic Payments

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Vibe-coding in ABAP via an MCP Server with OpenAI Codex
entry.content:
@HarshTR  Honestly, I don't do these things manually anymore. I just ask the AI to do it. So in this case I would tell Codex someting like: "Here is the URL of a GitHub repository which contains an MCP server that can be used to connect to SAP systems and edit objects there. I need you to install it on my VPS and configure it so that you can use it with my ABAP Trial 2023 system which runs in a Docker container on the same VPS. Please ask me for the info that you need to be able to do this".

It might not do it right on the first try (or it might - Codex today is much more advanced than the one I had available 4 months ago), but then you just give it the error message(s) that came up and eventually it figures it out and installs / configures everything correctly.

entry.title: Re: Is MDM actually worth it?
entry.content: Great point Corrie. AI Context = Transactional Data + Master Data. Equipping AI solely with the current and historical activity of the business seems to leave a grounding gap for AI. The article you reference makes me wonder if AI without solid master data collapses like a house of cards missing the center supports ... Or ... is the margin of error actually acceptable?

entry.title: Re: How do I grant a Datasphere Open SQL Schema user read...
entry.content:
No go. Security. No Tables can be directly read via Open SQL. 

Workaround: 

- create CLI script: Creates Exposed Views from Tables (naming convention will help to identify the tables properly). Python. 

- create DB Procedure: Get all Exposable Objects and Grant Select. SQL

- run each hour f.e. 

This way you fully automated the process.

entry.title: Smarter, More Flexible, More Powerful: SAP Analytics Cloud Composites Innovation Roundup
entry.content:
Composites are keeping up with the pace of innovation in SAP Analytics Cloud — and honestly, I think the last 18 months are proof of that. We've shipped meaningful capabilities across every single quarter: governance features that make composite libraries manageable at scale, scripting improvements that give developers real flexibility, and integrations that extend composites well beyond the boundaries of SAC itself. The momentum is real, and it's not slowing down.

I would like to walk you through the highlights from 2025 Q1 through 2026 Q2, so you can quickly see what's new and how it may change the way your team builds and consumes analytics content.

 2026 Q2  · Composite Versioning and Others

Version Control for Composite Artefacts

Perhaps the most anticipated governance feature for composite designers: version history. Designers can now manage up to 10 versions of a composite. The current version remains active in any consuming story, while previous versions can be opened in read-only mode for review — without touching anything live. If a past version needs to be restored, a backup of the current version is automatically created before the rollback takes effect.

All versions — current and historical — always reference the latest state of models and shared resources, and transports between tenants carry only the active version. This feature fundamentally changes how teams can iterate on composites in production environments.

Interface Properties in Composite Calculations

Dynamic analytics scenarios — think threshold-based visualizations or configurable reference lines — can now be built using calculations that reference composite interface properties, with no scripting required. Story designers set those values per composite properties so a single composite can support multiple scenarios through configuration alone. Development effort drops, reuse goes up.

Composites in Lite Viewer

Composites are now fully supported in Lite Viewer, enabling faster story loading for users  — making composite-based content more accessible across the organization.

Importing SAP-Delivered Composites from Intelligent Applications

Users can now import SAP-delivered composites from intelligent applications in SAP Business Data Cloud directly into their own stories. This means your team can build custom stories on top of SAP's vetted composite components, benefiting from ongoing SAP updates while retaining the freedom to extend and customize.

Deep Copy Support for Composites with Data Models

During installation of SAP-managed intelligent applications, composite widgets and data models are replicated across the package, enabling reuse across multiple stories from a single installation. 

 See it in Action:

 

2026 Q1  ·  Scripting Improvements and Designer Experience

Optional Arguments in Composite Interface Functions

Designers can now define optional arguments in composite interface functions, appended after mandatory ones. When a script function is auto-exposed, changes to its optional arguments are automatically synced with the interface function. When new optional arguments are added, existing consuming stories require no adjustments — backward compatibility is preserved automatically.

Overwriting Composites with the Same Technical ID

Composite designers can now save a modified composite back over the original technical ID, preserving identity in the system while evolving the content. Consuming stories must be re-opened and re-saved to pick up the change — a small but meaningful quality-of-life improvement.

Auto-Incorporation of Composite Data Sources

When a composite is inserted onto the story canvas, its associated data sources are now added automatically — limited to sources the user has access to and that haven't already been added. This eliminates the prior manual step and reduces the risk of designer error during story assembly.

Chart Colors and Patterns Carry Over from Composites

Chart colors and patterns defined within a composite are now preserved in consuming stories, removing a manual reconciliation step and ensuring that composite-level design intent is carried through end-to-end.

For details you can refer to:

What's New in SAP Analytics Cloud | Deep Dive with Product Experts | Q1 2026

2025 Q4  ·  Discoverability and Script Reuse

Where-Used List for Composites

A where-used list is now available in the file repository and at composite design time, showing which stories consume a given composite along with name, description, owner, creation date, and modification metadata. Columns are sortable and the list is searchable — making composite lifecycle management tractable at enterprise scale.

Automatic Exposure of Script Object Functions

Custom script object functions can now be automatically exposed as reusable interface functions, eliminating the need to manually copy scripts across stories when sharing logic between composites and consuming stories.

 For details you can refer to:

What's New in SAP Analytics Cloud | Deep Dive with Product Experts | Q4 2025

2025 Q3  ·  Scripting Depth and Design-Time Flexibility

Script Access for Composite Properties

Composite properties can now be read and assigned in the script editor and calculation editor, with errors and reference lists supported — bringing composite property scripting to parity with how other object properties are handled in the SAC scripting environment.

Page-Size Setting Improvements

Composite design-time page dimensions are now configurable across a wide range: width from 128px to 7680px, and height from 32px to 7680px. For composites that need to fit precisely into specific layout contexts, this granularity matters.

 For details you can refer to:

What's New in SAP Analytics Cloud | Deep Dive with Product Experts | Q3 2025

2025 Q2  ·  Visual Fidelity and UX Alignment

Threshold Support Within Composite Definitions

Visual thresholds can now be defined and embedded within the composite itself, operating independently from story-level thresholds — no risk of cross-composite interference. The initial scope covers chart widgets with measure-based thresholds; table and dimension-based assigned color support is planned for subsequent releases.

Fixed vs. Dynamic Composite Size

A new fixed sizing mode joins the existing dynamic default, giving designers pixel-precise control over composite dimensions for layout-critical scenarios where consistency across different story canvases is required.

Unified Styling and Builder Panel Experience

The composite properties panel has been integrated into the Styling panel, and composite model configuration has been moved into the Builder panel — aligning composites with the interaction pattern used for charts and tables, and reducing cognitive overhead for story designers who work across widget types.

 For details you can refer to:

What's New in SAP Analytics Cloud | Deep Dive with Product Experts | Q2 2025

2025 Q1  ·  Foundational Capability Expansion

Global Calculations in Composite Widgets

Account, dimension, and cross-calculations can now be defined directly within composite widgets. Custom widgets with applicable calculation support are also covered. This closes a significant capability gap that had limited how analytically rich a self-contained composite could be.

Flow Layout Panel and Slider Widgets

The composite widget palette now includes Flow Layout Panel, Slider, and Range Slider. Flow Layout Panel enables composites that adapt gracefully to different screen sizes. Sliders enable interactive, dynamic composites — for instance, what-if simulation scenarios where users adjust inputs and see results update in real time.

Linked Analysis Support for Composite Target Widgets

Composites can now participate in linked analysis as target widgets. When a source widget drives a filter or selection, composite widgets — and the charts, tables, custom widgets, and text elements within them that share the same data source — will respond accordingly.

Option to Disable Model Configuration in Composites

A new Global Settings option lets composite owners lock down data model configuration, preventing story designers from substituting models when consistency must be enforced. It's unchecked by default, preserving existing flexibility.

 For details you can refer to:

What's New in SAP Analytics Cloud | Deep Dive with Product Experts | Q1 2025

Summary and Outlook

Stepping back across these 6 quarters of release notes, three clear themes emerge:

  • Governance and lifecycle management — Versioning, where-used lists, transport controls, and deep copy support are progressively turning composites from ad hoc reuse artifacts into properly governed, enterprise-grade content components.
  • Developer and designer experience — Optional arguments, auto-exposure of script functions, auto-incorporation of data sources, and unified panel UX are all reducing friction and aligning composites with the quality-of-life expectations of modern analytics developers.
  • Integration with the broader SAP ecosystem — Lite Viewer support, SAP Business Data Cloud intelligent application imports, and Datasphere model integration are broadening composites capabilities across the entire SAP Business Data Cloud, with full support for Datasphere models and an expanding set of intelligent applications

For analytics teams building on SAP Analytics Cloud, these innovations collectively lower the cost of building reusable content, raise the ceiling of what composites can express, and provide the governance infrastructure needed to manage composite libraries at enterprise scale.

We are continuously advancing composite capabilities through ongoing innovation. Please stay tuned for further updates !

entry.title: One Batch. Zero Guesswork. That's SAP Digital Manufacturing for Process Industries.
entry.content:
Introduction

Process manufacturing is unforgiving. A batch of carbonated beverage doesn't wait for the right information to arrive. It either runs right, on spec, on time, fully traceable, or it doesn't. And when it doesn't, the cost shows up everywhere: in scrap, in delayed orders, in quality holds, and in the hours spent reconstructing what actually happened on the shop floor.

Most plants are still managing that complexity with a combination of ERP systems that don't reach the floor, paper-based work instructions, manual quality logs, and OEE reports that arrive a day too late to act on. It works until it doesn't.

SAP Digital Manufacturing is built for exactly this environment. Here's what a single production shift looks like when everything is connected.

Everything Connected

It starts at 6 AM on the shop floor. A thousand liters of carbonated beverage need to be mixed, bottled, quality-checked, and shipped today.

The order arrives on the shop floor the moment it's released. When the demand planner creates a process order in SAP S/4HANA, it flows instantly into SAP Digital Manufacturing, with the recipe, the batch, the schedule, and an automatically-created inspection lot already attached. The production supervisor releases it in a single click.

No emails. No manual handoff. No gap between plan and execution.

The operator always knows exactly what to do. Helena, the production operator, opens her Mixing POD and sees her assigned orders, the phases to execute, and the work instructions including cleaning procedures and process diagrams, embedded directly in the screen.

She selects the right raw material batch, records process parameters, and the system validates every value against tolerance limits in real time. If a primary ingredient is short, it surfaces the approved alternates automatically. Nothing is left to memory or improvisation.

Quality is confirmed in the process, not at the end.

As Helena completes the mixing phase, she records CO2 content, temperature, water purity, and Brix directly against the inspection lot, right in the POD, with no separate system to open.

Each value is checked against spec instantly. By the time the batch moves to bottling, the quality record is already built. The quality manager sees a summarized valuation, all characteristics in range, and makes the usage decision in seconds, not hours.

When something goes wrong, the right people know immediately. During bottling phase, a machine goes down. The operator logs the downtime with reason code and duration directly in the Bottling POD and raises an alert to the supervisor with a single tap.

Meanwhile, the Line Monitor shows the plant manager in real time that availability has dropped, what the top scrap reason codes are, and where OEE losses are concentrated. Not in tomorrow's report. Now.

When the batch is done, everything is already traceable.

SAP Digital Manufacturing writes every confirmation, every goods movement, and every quality result back to S/4HANA automatically.

The Product Genealogy Report is available on demand: which sugar batch went in, what the phosphoric acid quantity was, what Brix value was recorded, when the finished goods batch was receipted. If a recall question arrives, the answer takes seconds, not days.

That's a full shift, plan to fulfilled product, with no paper, no manual reconciliation, and no guesswork.

Conclusion

For process industry manufacturers, this kind of end-to-end connection between planning, execution, quality, and traceability is what separates plants that react from plants that perform. It's not about replacing people on the shop floor. It's about giving every person, the demand planner, the production supervisor, the operator, the quality manager, the plant manager, exactly the information they need, exactly when they need it, with no friction in between.

The result is faster, cleaner production. Fewer quality holds. OEE data that drives real decisions. And a batch genealogy that's always ready for auditors, for regulators, and for customers who need to know their product was made right.

That's what SAP Digital Manufacturing delivers for process industries. And it starts with one batch, running right.

 

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.
Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: Hide Award Configuration Fields from Workflow Approval Page of Reward and Recognition
entry.content: @xavierlegarrec – Thank you very much for reviewing the question and providing a quick suggestion. I tested the option in the workflow to set “Respect Permission = Yes” at each workflow level. While this works well for most standard workflows, it does not fully fit the Spot Awards scenario. In Spot Awards, we have the following role requirements: Nominee (Employee Role): Needs access to Spot Award and Program for viewing Nominator Role: Needs access to Spot Award, Budget, and Program to view for their subordinates Because permissions are granted at the MDF level, the approver is often either a Nominee or Nominator, which makes the permission behavior complex. Additionally, our workflow includes: Manager’s Manager HR Manager In many cases: The initiator and final approver are the same person, so they must have access to initiate (and therefore can view the workflow) The Manager’s Manager level may also overlap with existing roles For the HRBP role, this setup might work; however, if the HRBP is also acting as a Nominator, they will still have visibility Because of these overlapping roles and MDF-based permissions, the “Respect Permission” setting does not fully restrict visibility as expected in this scenario.

entry.title: Re: Hide Award Configuration Fields from Workflow Approval Page of Reward and Recognition
entry.content:
@xavierlegarrec – Thank you very much for reviewing the question and providing a quick suggestion

I tested the option in the workflow to set “Respect Permission = Yes” at each workflow level. While this works well for most standard workflows, it does not fully fit the Spot Awards scenario.

In Spot Awards, we have the following role requirements:

  • Nominee (Employee Role): Needs access to Spot Award and Program for viewing
  • Nominator Role: Needs access to Spot Award, Budget, and Program to view for their subordinates

Because permissions are granted at the MDF level, the approver is often either a Nominee or Nominator, which makes the permission behavior complex.

Additionally, our workflow includes:

  1. Manager’s Manager
  2. HR
  3. Manager

In many cases:

  • The initiator and final approver are the same person, so they must have access to initiate (and therefore can view the workflow)
  • The Manager’s Manager level may also overlap with existing roles
  • For the HRBP role, this setup might work; however, if the HRBP is also acting as a Nominator, they will still have visibility

Because of these overlapping roles and MDF-based permissions, the “Respect Permission” setting does not fully restrict visibility I feel.

entry.title: Re: Adding custom field in "Receive Products" app
entry.content:
Dear @Pyae_Phyo_Thwe,

The Receive Products app does not provide a released standard extensibility option to add an arbitrary custom field or custom button directly into the delivered SAP UI. SAP Business Application Studio or Eclipse/ADT cannot be used to modify the SAP-delivered standard app directly. They can only be used for released extensibility scenarios, such as a separate custom or side-by-side app, an adaptation project where supported or developer extensibility based on released APIs, BAdIs and predefined extension points

More details below:

SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library – Receive Products
SAP Help – Creating Custom Fields
SAP Help – Developer Extensibility
SAP Learning – Developer Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

As a clean-core workaround the recommended approach is to build a separate companion app or tile and link it to the relevant receiving object, for example purchase order, inbound delivery or material document. If the requirement is to have the field or button directly inside the standard Receive Products app this should be raised as an SAP Customer Influence request

More details below:

SAP KBA 2963059 – How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2026: Registration is live!
entry.content:
SAP TechEd’s 30th anniversary!

Can you believe that SAP TechEd turns thirty this year?  Let’s party like it’s 1996 (portable CD Discman anyone?!)  In honor of all the great TechEd moments, we’ll take a trip down memory lane with you, celebrating TechEd’s legacy as the premier technical event for developers in the past, the present, and especially into the future.

We’ll be back in Berlin this October with the full-fledged, in-person, old school, TechEd experience you know and love. So, what’s in the works?  You name it: Hands-on Workshops, Strategy Talks, Road Maps, Deep Dives, Demo Stations and my favorite, a full-blown Community Clubhouse with fan favorites like the Developer Garage and the Community Theater. Berlin is your place to connect with SAP experts, Developer Advocates and your TechEd friends and neighbors in the flesh! TechEd fans rejoice!

What if I’m looking for more of a “sweatpants and sofa” vibe?

Fear not, my friend!  Throughout the three days, we'll be livestreaming sessions from Berlin for our live virtual show.  Many of our in-person Berlin sessions will be streamed live and available on-demand, and we’ll also have a virtual Q&A experience, where experts field questions from both live and virtual audiences.  In fact, much of our virtual show will take place from our virtual studio right on the Berlin show floor, with exclusive segments designed for our virtual audience.  

What kinds of sessions can I expect?

Great question.  We'll have different session formats so you can choose how you prefer to learn. Want to sit back and hear all about SAP strategy from our experts?  Or are you looking to get your hands dirty with some coding?  Are you looking for a high-level overview or want to dive deep into a particular topic? The choice is totally yours.

As we all know, at the end of the day, SAP TechEd is about community above all else, and we’ve got so much planned to bring us together in Berlin:

Come hang with us!

It's all about connecting, learning, and celebrating our community. It’s SAP TechEd and we’re beyond pumped and honored to bring it all to you this October! Register today and let me know what you are looking forward to the most.

entry.title: Re: Manage Workforce App: Employee Extension to Customer...
entry.content: Hi Michal, Thank you for the detailed explanation and references. I agree that the standard Public Cloud workforce model is based on the Employee BP (BUP003) and Employment BP (BUP010), and that Customer and Supplier roles should be assigned to the Employment BP rather than the Employee BP. However, the challenges we are facing appear to be beyond the general BP model itself. 1. Employment BP cannot be used in standard SD processing We have already followed SAP Note 3464354 and successfully extended Employment BPs to the FI Customer role (FLCU00). The issue arises when attempting to extend the same Employment BP to the SD Customer role (FLCU01). Our findings are: Employment BPs are created correctly through the workforce process. FLCU00 can be assigned. FLCU01 cannot be maintained in a usable way for SD processing. Standard partner determination configuration required for SD is not delivered for account group EMPL. Required partner functions (Sold-To, Ship-To, Bill-To, Payer, etc.) are not available. Even when attempting to expose partner functions through available configuration, the system prevents saving because corresponding partner determination entries are missing and cannot be maintained in Public Cloud. As a result, the Employment BP cannot be used as a Sold-To Party in standard sales orders despite SAP documentation indicating that customer roles should reside on the Employment BP. Therefore, the question is not whether Customer roles belong on Employment BPs. We agree they do. The question is: If SAP's recommended model is to assign Customer roles to Employment BPs, why does standard Public Cloud not deliver the SD partner determination configuration required to actually execute Sales Orders using those Employment BPs? This appears to be a functional gap between the BP model and the SD execution layer. 2. Mass Maintenance app does not recognize Employment BPs We are also encountering a separate issue with app F7974 (Manage Mass Maintenance - BP). Although Employment BPs are valid business partners and are extended to Customer and Supplier roles, the application returns: "No records found for this selection" when Employment BP numbers are entered. The behavior suggests that the application may only be searching specific BP categories or account groups and is excluding Employment BPs. The question here is: Is F7974 intended to support Employment BPs (BUP010), particularly when they are extended as Customer or Supplier business partners? If not, what is SAP's recommended standard mass-maintenance mechanism for workforce-linked BPs? Feature Request Clarification Regarding the enhancement request, I would slightly disagree that the gap is only a missing unified maintenance UI. The underlying BP relationship already exists, but the current limitation is functional: Employment BPs can be extended to FI Customer. SAP documentation suggests Customer roles should exist on Employment BPs. Standard SD processing cannot be executed because required SD partner determination content is not delivered. Standard BP Mass Maintenance appears unable to process Employment BPs. Therefore, the requested enhancement is not primarily a UI improvement but rather completion of the end-to-end business process so that workforce-generated Employment BPs can participate fully in standard Customer and SD scenarios without custom configuration or duplicate master data. Could you confirm whether SAP currently supports: Employment BP (BUP010) + FLCU01 + standard SD Sales Orders in Public Cloud? F7974 Manage Mass Maintenance for Employment BPs extended as Customer/Supplier? If these scenarios are not supported today, then I believe the customer influence request remains valid. Best regards, Ansil

entry.title: Re: #COE#RFL01- Change company code currency from EUR to VND
entry.content:
Dear @MounaAndre,

SAP does not provide a standard conversion option to change an already created company code from EUR to VND in Public Cloud. SAP confirms that company code currency cannot be changed, even through expert configuration. Therefore, if company code XX was created with EUR as company code/local currency the system will continue to generate and manage standard accounting values in EUR for example during balance carryforward. Using VND as transaction currency does not replace the company code/local currency

For completeness: functional currency and additional currency types available in Public Cloud are not a solution for this case. They are assigned in addition to the company code currency, so they do not replace company code currency type 10. As a result, the legal/local company code currency remains EUR and EUR balances and balance carryforward continue to be generated. They may support parallel reporting scenarios, but they do not make VND the company code/local currency. This can only be achieved by creating a new company code

The recommended clean-core solution is to create a new company code with VND as the company code currency and perform a controlled cutover/migration. This new company code must be created under the Vietnam country version, so that VND is the local currency and the Vietnamese localization (including SAP Document and Reporting Compliance) applies. The existing EUR company code should be retained for historical reporting and audit purposes.

If SAP standard does not meet the business requirement the correct channel is to submit or vote for an improvement request in SAP Customer Influence

More details below:

SAP KBA 2812802 – Request to change the company code currency from local currency to other currency – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP Help – Balance Carryforward Status

SAP Help – Create Additional Currency Types

SAP KBA 2963059 – How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP Help - Define Functional Currency

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: #COE#RFL01- Change company code currency from EUR to VND

entry.title: Re: Push Notifications Not Displayed When MDK App Is Open...
entry.content:
The tutorial really focuses on the necessary configuration to enable receipt of the notifications.  Most applications may also want to provide additional logic around the push notifications. This is accomplished through the use of the three OnReceive handler events in Application,app 

  • OnReceiveFetchCompletion - called when a push is received and 
    • For iOS, the request contains { "apns.contentAvailable": true }
    • For Android, the request does not contain any notification section
  • OnReceiveForegroundNotification - called when a push is received and your application is in the foreground
  • OnReceiveNotificationResponse - called when the app is not in the foreground and the user selected the push notification

You can assign a rule to one or all of these

entry.title: Re: Werb Service PW-SELF-SERV missing in system
entry.content: Dear Shujah, to help you out. SAP may need to know which product that service WD_PW_SELF_SERV is from? From the naming convention "WD" it is a Web Dynpro App. Try to find your definition in either Tcode SE80 or find your authorization under tcode PFCG what role is this assign to? You need to give more context? This is a question on why it is not found. Missing service could be that it was not installed, or the System Alias where this service was copied from is pointing to the wrong system alias (RFC - SM59). Find that service /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE and check the System Alias used by that service and check your RFC Tcode - SM59 if it exist? SICF will not be there if the service is not there from what I understand.

entry.title: Re: selective import charm
entry.content:
Hi,

Regarding your concern, the answer is No. You cannot select the TR directly, but need to select the related document, this is by design.

And from document below, we don't recommend to use selective import strategy directly, due to reason:

"Selecting change documents individually can affect the transport request import sequence, because the import buffers of the follow-on systems are modified. If the transported change documentss are related to each other, or have interdependencies, we do not recomment that you import selectively."

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Solution_Manager/8b923a2175be4939816f0981b73856c7/61e7f3c065a54a7db584e22936d8e97e.html

If you still want to use it, kindly pay attention to above notes.

Or you could use Status-Dependent and Selective Import strategy as an alternative.

BR

entry.title: The Architecture Question Your AI Strategy Can't Avoid
entry.content:
IDEA Forum - Copenhagen, 2025

The most common architectural response to an AI readiness problem is the wrong one: consolidate everything into a single system, run AI on top of it, declare the foundation solved.

It doesn't work.

Large organizations don't have a consolidation problem. They have a connection problem. A customer exists differently in three systems. A product code means one thing in finance and something adjacent in supply chain. That fragmentation is structural,  built over years of acquisitions and org changes. Centralizing it would take years and introduce a single point of failure.

The right answer isn't to move the data. It's to connect it.

---

## What the framework points to

The ODI's Framework for AI-Ready Enterprise Data,  publishing this month through the IDEA programme, defines 21 concrete criteria across four pillars. Read across them, and a pattern emerges. The requirements don't point toward centralization. They point toward connection: connecting distributed data without moving it, enriching it with semantic context, governing it in a way that holds for autonomous agents as well as humans.

That pattern has a name: a data fabric.

A data fabric is not a product, there's no software you install and declare yourself AI-ready. It's an architectural approach: integration pipelines, active catalogues, and governance engines working together to make distributed data usable. It leaves the data where it is and creates a unified virtual access layer on top - harmonizing contents at the point of query, surfacing a verified, governed response to whoever or whatever is asking.

The ODI's supplementary research on data fabrics, released alongside the main framework, maps this to three specific capabilities: integration, cataloguing, and governance.

---

## Why it matters for SAP organizations specifically

Each of those three capabilities has a direct read-across to the SAP data landscape.

Integration is where entity resolution lives, continuous matching across connected systems, merging fragmented records into the golden records AI can actually trust. SAP Business Data Cloud operationalizes this for the SAP ecosystem, harmonizing data objects across S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, Ariba, and the broader application landscape into governed, consistent business entities.

Cataloguing is where most organizations are further behind than they think. A static data catalog is a directory, useful for humans, insufficient for AI. What the framework calls for is active cataloguing: machine learning continuously profiling data as it arrives, tracking lineage, building a semantic map that AI agents can traverse to understand not just what data exists, but how everything relates.

Governance is the capability that scales worst without intentional architecture. Traditional governance was built for humans who read policies and exercise judgment. AI agents don't do that. The framework's answer is policy-as-code: access rules enforced at the infrastructure level, before the model ever sees the data.

---

## The timing

The framework publishes this month. On June 25th, the ODI team (Elena Simperl, Paul Groth, and Stuart Coleman) will present it live and take questions. One hour, free.

[Register for the June 25th webinar]

And if you want early access to the full report before it's publicly indexed:

[Join IDEA]

---

The organizations that figure out the connection problem are the ones that will scale AI reliably. Everyone else will keep running pilots.

Where does your organization sit? Have you started the architectural conversation, or is it still downstream of the model selection debate?

---

IDEA — the Interchange for Data and Enterprise AI — is a programme of independent research and peer learning developed in partnership between SAP and the Open Data Institute. Nine roundtables. 140+ senior executives. Three research briefs. And a framework that changes how enterprise AI readiness gets measured.

Since Vacant is a Boolean field, it returns values as True/False. However, I’ve noticed that it is categorised under Measures rather than Dimensions, which prevents it from being directly used in an input control.

I attempted a workaround by creating a calculated column to convert the Boolean value into a string (e.g., “Vacant” / “Not Vacant”), but this approach didn’t work as expected for filtering purposes.

My goal is to apply a filter so that the report displays only records where:

Vacant = True

Has anyone encountered this limitation or found an effective way to enable filtering on Boolean fields in Story Reports—particularly through input controls or alternative design approaches?

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance,

Harini

entry.title: SAP Xperience CWB 2026: Conexão, Aprendizado Prático e a Força da Nossa Comunidade!
entry.content:
Olá, comunidade SAP! 💙💛

O SAP Xperience CWB 2026 foi um verdadeiro sucesso e deixou uma marca incrível em nossa comunidade local. Foi uma manhã intensa de muito aprendizado, troca de experiências, conexões habilidades e, acima de tudo, muita colaboração dentro do ecossistema SAP.



 

Esta foto oficial representa muito mais do que um encontro: ela reflete a energia de pessoas dispostas a aprender, compartilhar conhecimento e crescer juntas. Agradecemos sinceramente a cada participante pela dedicação, presença e pela atmosfera inspirada que criamos juntos!

 



 

🚀Teoria e Prática Caminhando Juntas

Sabemos que a teoria é importante, mas é na prática que o verdadeiro aprendizado acontece. Durante o evento, os participantes literalmente colocaram a "mão na massa"! Eles exploraram o sistema, trocaram insights em tempo real e vivenciaram o universo SAP de forma prática, interativa e colaborativa. Foi incrível acompanhar o engajamento, a evolução e o brilho nos olhos de cada um que esteve presente no laboratório.

 



🧠 Dedicação que Inspira: Nossos Instrutores

Ver o conhecimento sendo transmitido de forma tão generosa é o que nos dá orgulho de fazer parte da SAP Community Curitiba. Por isso, deixamos o nosso muito obrigado a Luiz Carlos de Souza e Jhean Lanski . Eles dedicaram voluntariamente seu tempo, energia e expertise técnica para guiar os participantes ao longo de toda uma atividade hands-on, impulsionando uma jornada de novos talentos e inspirando a todos nós.

 



 

🎬O Time de Bastidores: Quem Fez Acontecer

Por trás de um grande evento, há sempre um tempo incrível trabalhando com paixão. Queremos expressar nossa profunda gratidão às voluntárias que cuidaram de cada detalhe com tanto carinho e dedicação:

  • Simone Azevedo , que liderou com maestria todo o planejamento e a organização do evento.

  • Beatriz Sakai e Bruna Gritti Brizola , que no dia do evento comandaram os bastidores, garantindo que tudo corresse perfeitamente na Escola Politécnica da PUCPR.

     

Obrigado por doarem seu tempo e talento. Vocês fizeram toda a diferença!

💙Nossa Gratidão aos Apoiadores

Iniciativas como essas só saem do papel quando encontramos parceiros que acreditam no poder da comunidade. Nossos agradecimentos especiais às empresas e projetos que nos promoveram e caminharam conosco nessa jornada:

  • CHpro Solutions

  • Escola Politécnica PUCPR

  • Lab2Learn | SAP BTP

  • Moovi

  • Abap para meninas

Vocês são parte fundamental do ecossistema e do fortalecimento da nossa comunidade local. Obrigado por acreditarem neste propósito!

Nos vemos nos próximos eventos! 🚀💙💛

🎯Organização: Simone Azevedo | Luiz Carlos de Souza | Adriano Marques de Oliveira

#ComunidadeSAP #ComunidadeSAPCWB #SAPXperienceCWB #SAPXPCWB

entry.title: Re: WFA Empty Screen After OLAP Success – Fact Table Jobs...
entry.content:
Hi @ricardosuarez 

The default of the landing page was set to a blank page.  The reason is that if someone has RBP issue or customer support, they can still go to Workforce Analytics and can go to the Admin in Workforce Analytics.  If the landing page is setup with KPI dashboard, user with RBP issue or customer support won't be able to go in at all.

Since you are expecting to see something, I've changed the default setting to KPI dashboard.  Please check. 

Also, do you still have issue not able to see any measures on the left-hand side menu?  If you still have this issue, please create support ticket, they should be able to help with functional issue.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP4ME change IT contact email and Name
entry.content:
As per your description, I understand you would like to change IT contact person for SAP Ariba Suite.

However, our support team does not have the authority to directly assist the customer in updating the IT contact person. The IT contact person is usually designated by the customer based on the provided information when signing the relevant cloud product contract. If the customer needs to change the IT contact person, they need to contact their Account Manager according to the KBA 2991636 - How to change the Main IT Contact/Technical Administrator for an SAP BTP Global Account to process this, which is also done with the consideration of protecting the customer's data security.

I have found the Account Manager contact information for you as follows. Please contact him for further processing.

entry.title: Re: The issue of program RK_CYCLE_UPLOAD_DOWNLOAD
entry.content:
Dear Liangnan,

Thank you for the information.

I have checked the available SAP documentation. In allocation cycle maintenance, the receiver rule “Variable Portions” is supported for receiver tracing factors. SAP documentation also explains that, when this receiver rule is used, the system determines the allocation base based on the maintained variable portion method.

However, for transaction KSEUD / Excel upload-download, the upload is handled through a predefined template and specific upload logic. Based on the behavior you reported, it appears that the tracing factor “Variable Investments” may not be accepted by the upload program, even though it may be possible to maintain it manually in the cycle.

Unfortunately, I could not see the attachment from my side. Therefore, I am not able to fully verify the exact error message or the uploaded template.

If you need further assistance, I would recommend opening a case with SAP Support and attaching the relevant screenshots, upload file, cycle details, and exact error message. We will then look into the issue further from the system/program side.

Thank you for your understanding.

Best regards,
Nghia

entry.title: Re: Custom copy description program between languages
entry.content:
Hello Maria, 

This behavior is covered by standard SAP design. Cost center descriptions are language-dependent and are stored separately per logon language. Therefore, a description maintained in English is not automatically available in Spanish or other languages.

You can refer to https://me.sap.com/notes/0003458548 - How to maintain cost center texts in language dependent

I hope this helps

entry.title: Re: Reallocation cycle in allocating to Overheads line
entry.content:
Hello Ramadevi,

The most likely reason is a difference in allocation/reporting setup or master data between company code 0716 and 071C. GR55 only displays the values based on the report definition, while the reallocation cycle only clears the sender values that are included in the cycle definition. If some cost centers or cost elements are included in the GR55 Overheads line but are not included in the reallocation cycle sender selection for company code 0716, the Overheads line will not become zero.

I hope this helps!

entry.title: SAP Asset Performance Management-Manage Rules and Cumulocity From a Business Perspective
entry.content:
This blog outlines how to trigger an "Alert" and a "Maintenance Notification" within SAP Asset Performance Management(APM) by ingesting time series data via the underlying Embedded IoT platform (Cumulocity). It also covers how to navigate to the Digital Twin , Device Management, and Cockpit of the SAP APM Embedded IoT Platform using "Manage Devices" and  "Map Devices to Technical Objects" applications within SAP APM.

If you want to gain a deep understanding of a few SAP Asset Performance Management (APM) functionalities, check out my SAP APM blog posts links below.

  1. SAP Asset Performance Management Meets SAP Situation Handling - Extended Framework 
  2. SAP Asset Performance Management - Transport Management : Import Transport Requests to Target Tenant 
  3. SAP Asset Performance Management - Ingesting Time Series Data - Derivation & Monitor Rule Management 
  4. SAP Asset Performance Management - Visual Asset Health Monitoring (Part-1) 
  5. SAP Asset Performance Management - Manage Embedded IoT Metadata Sync 
  6. What`s New in SAP Asset Performance Management - Release 2502 
  7. Achieving Sustainability With SAP Asset Performance Management By Leveraging Industrial Systems. 
  8. Configuring Failure Curve Analytics (FCA) In SAP Asset Performance Management (APM)

 

My Business Use Case:

My calcination plant at "Castrop-Rauxel" wants to monitor Vibrations at Drive End for one of the Cooling Water Circulation Pumps, and trigger alerts if the vibration readings deviate from the threshold values. From the business perspective, I have created  three local Indicators in SAP APM representing vibrations at Axial, Horizontal, and Vertical positions for the Cooling Water Circulation Pump Drive End.

Disclaimer : For this demonstration, I have chosen smartphone application capabilities. When we use smartphone wizard for device registration, smartphone is automatically registered by Cumulocity.

Let us first create Cooling Water Circulation Pump-01 (CWCP01) in S/4HANA.

SAP Asset Performance Management (APM) has a two-layered synchronization model:

  1. ERP system to SAP Asset Performance Management (External Synchronization) - This step synchronizes master data, configuration, and transactional data from S/4HANA or SAP ECC into SAP APM.
  2. SAP Asset Performance Management to Embedded IoT (Internal Synchronization) - In this step, technical objects are synchronized from SAP APM to the Embedded IoT layer. This synchronization runs asynchronously as soon as the technical objects and indicators become available in SAP APM.

Once the data is replicated into SAP APM, let us create "Local Indicators" for monitoring Cooling Water Circulation Pump-01 (CWCP01) drive end vibrations at Axial, Horizontal, and Vertical positions.

Cooling Water Circulation Pump-01 Local Indicators

 

Now let us mimic the scenario by connecting the smartphone to "CWCP01". An acceleration sensor is used to detect CWCP01 Drive End Vibrations. A Triaxial Sensor which has three independent internal sensing elements for X, Y, and Z axes.

 

Map Devices To Technical Objects:

Navigate to the Digital Twin Manager by selecting the Map Devices to Technical Objects application from SAP APM. Technical Objects are synchronized from SAP APM to the Embedded IoT platform as Digital Twins.

Map Devices To Technical Objects

Digital Twin is a virtual representation of a physical object or system. It is typically used in the context of the Internet of Things (IoT) to help visualize and analyze how a physical object is performing. In the Digital Twin Manager Application, one asset represents one Digital Twin.

In the Digital Twin Manager, we can view all the technical objects and indicators that have been onboarded to SAP Asset Performance Management.

Child technical objects associated with superordinate technical object.

Let us view the Cooling Water Circulation Pump-01 local indicators created in SAP APM under the  "Indicators" tab in the Digital Twin Manager.

Let us link the devices from the Embedded IoT platform with the corresponding Indicators in SAP APM.t us Technical Object Indicators can be linked The "source" identifies the unique ID of the device that is generating the measurement. The status "Linked" means that the indicator is successfully connected to a designated source (managed object/device), and indicates source is fully operational. .

 

 

Mini Note: Ensure that the Unit Of Measure (UoM) of the time series data from the device of the Embedded IoT platform matches with the corresponding SAP APM Indicator UoM.

 

Manage Devices:

The Manage Devices application in the SAP Asset Performance Management (APM) navigates users to Device Management in the Embedded IoT platform. Device Management plays a vital role in building a robust and scalable foundation for IoT solution. It bridges the gap between IoT devices and the business application layer. The devices list contains all devices that are connected to the tenant.

Devices can be grouped according to the business needs. As per my use case, I have created multiple groups to represent Technical Object Type, and assigned devices to groups.

The dash board manager in device management application: 

The measurement tab provides a default visualization of numeric data provided by the device in the form of charts. The screenshot below displays a chart for Cooling Water Circulation Pump Drive End Vibrations with three different series, such as Horizontal, Vertical, and Axial.

 

Since we have linked the device with the SAP APM Indicators, the data flows in and vibration readings for cooling water circulation pump can be viewed in SAP APM Indicator Monitoring Tab.

 

Manage Rules:

To support my business use case, I have set up monitoring rules for the CWCP01 DE sensor in SAP APM. These monitoring rules triggers an alert & notification if the vibration readings deviate from the threshold values.

Monitoring Rules : CWCP01 DE Vibrations (Axial, Horizontal, Vertical)

For example, the rule "CWCP01_DE_AX" condition:

Once the rule executes, If the condition is met, the executed rule triggers an alert and creates a notification. Since the CWCP01 DE Axial vibration reading is > 1mm/s, the executed rule has created an alert and a notification.

 

 

Monitor Alerts:

Now, let us view the triggered alerts in the "Monitor Alerts" application.

 

 

Cockpit:

The cockpit application provides options to manage and monitor IoT assets data from a business perspective, including visualizing data in dashboards and managing reports.

Home Dashboard: This shows the data for the general tenant. It includes active critical alarms, quick links, recent alarms, Data Points, etc. Maintenance team can design the Home Dashboard according to their needs. The dashboard can be customized using "Edit Widgets" option.

Widgets in Dashboard and reports can display maps, images, graphs, tables. Widgets are useful to track information.

Working With Reports:

Reports enable tracking applications, alarms, assets, and other data in a dashboard layout. Reports are global dashboard pages regardless of the asset hierarchy.

In the reports page we can view all the configured reports with their names.

Let us now click on "Cooling Water Circulation Pump/Motor" report.

I have color coded the CWCP01 DE Vibration readings using Edit Widget option.

I have specified the Min. & Max. values for Yellow Range and Red Range.

Now the Data Point Table widget highlights the vibration readings whenever an exception occurs.

Red Range:  Minimum 1.1 mm/s & Maximum 2 mm/s 

Red Range

Yellow Range: Minimum 0.6 mm/s and Maximum 1 mm/s

Yellow Range

 

😊This brings me to the end of the high level walkthrough of SAP Asset Performance Management Embedded IoT features : Digital Twin Manager, Device Management, Cockpit, and Monitoring Rule Management. I hope this blog proves useful to the Plant Maintenance team.

 

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy. C

entry.title: GRC Tuesdays: Digital Compliance Monitoring Cockpit with SAP Risk and Assurance Management
entry.content:
Enterprise Risk and Compliance cockpit image generated by AI

Leading risk and compliance in any organization can sometimes feel like flying a fighter jet straight into a storm with no way of avoiding it. And your mission is to ensure that the plane goes through it without damages and continues its course smoothly.

Daunting, right? But there are upsides: 1. You are not alone since the entire organization – including Senior Management, is onboard, and 2. Contrarily to early wooden planes, yours can benefit from instant data available at your fingertips. Preventing you from finding out that one tank is burst and fuel leaking – slowly but surely – only once the gauge turns to critical red…

 

What makes a good cockpit?

 

When looking at how engineers design plane cockpits, they consider multiple dimensions of course, but with one output in mind: it needs to deliver the right information, at the right time, in the simplest possible way – while enabling immediate action under stressful conditions.

How does that compare to SAP Risk and Assurance Management might you say? Well, let’s continue this analogy!

 

Situational awareness

 

The pilot – here the 2nd line of defense composed of Risk Managers, Compliance Officers, Cybersecurity leads, etc., must understand everything happening around them (compliance coverage, control gaps, cyber threats) instantly.

SAP Risk and Assurance Management helps achieves this by combining data from various systems to show a unified and coherent view of compliance status. Regardless of the compliance initiative: financial governance, IT Security, tax checks, etc.

Controls Overview dashboard app with view of various key metrics of controls

 

Information prioritization

 

At Mach 3, information overload leads to paralysis or worse even, mistakes. The same applies to an organization.

SAP Fiori apps in SAP Risk and Assurance Management have been specifically designed to display only critical data with easy to grasp symbology (icons, colours) to make it obvious what needs to be done.

Manage Risks app

 

Head-Up Display

 

Granted not many Compliance Officers have 3D helmet systems in their office to visualise their compliance progress – but that would be pretty cool, key data is directly in the line of sight with advanced reporting. No need to look at the ground – or Excel sheet – to know at what altitude you are.

Internal control, compliance and risk management information rendered in SAP Analytics Cloud

 

Hands-On Throttle-And-Stick

 

Maybe not as critical in an office than in a plane, but still, the office version of the stick controller is the… mighty computer mouse!

Less clicking, more doing. Critical details are available directly on the line records in the dedicated apps. This reduces the need to search for information so enables users to act rapidly and decisively.

My Controls app

 

High level of automation

 

A digital co-pilot, this is precisely what this is. Alleviating the workload so that Compliance and Risk specialists – but also Senior Management when using this information, can turn their attention to tactics and decision-making. As a result, the system must behave autonomously and manage non-critical tasks itself. These can include categorizing and closing false alerts so that users can spend effort on resolving real issues requiring attention and expertise.

Predicted Conclusions for Issue Management and Remediation

 

Integration of all systems

 

To achieve the automation mentioned in the previous paragraph, there is one prerequisite: everything must work as one system, not separate components. Only then can true automation take place. Navigation, communication, and sensors must be fully integrated so that there is no need to manually reconcile different sources.

Simplified diagram of integration points in SAP Risk and Assurance Management

 

Clear and immediate alert

 

As for a pilot, business process owners must react rapidly to threats and danger. Increasing risk level of course but also control deficiency fit well in this category. Early warnings with real time notification categorized by severity provide them with timely information to address the underlying issues. Alerts in the tool of course, but also sent to the beloved email inboxes.

Email notification with direct link to the record

 

Rapid drill-down capability

 

Should an alert be raised because a control has failed, it is critical to seamlessly transition from awareness to action. Users must be able to quickly navigate from an overview to the underlying details of the inconsistency and initiate the appropriate remediation steps without delay. This ability to navigate efficiently from insights to action ensures that risks are addressed promptly, and compliance is maintained in a continuous and controlled manner.

Direct access to the live source system data for root cause resolution

 

Reliability and redundancy

 

For external stakeholders, process checks performed in SAP Risk and Assurance Management might not be perceived as critical. But when they realize that these control checks relate to compliance, then the perception shifts. This information must remain available for auditors and regulators, but also for Senior Management to support their decision making and provide traceability in case of investigation. Here, backups and fail-safe help prevent failures.

In this area, “SAP cloud solutions provide contractually agreed-upon service-level agreements (SLAs) for resilience and recovery. Customers can enhance these baselines SLAs by selecting premium options appropriate to their business requirements and threat models, including high availability and disaster recovery deployment options” (Securing SAP Cloud Environments)

SAP Trust Center landing page

SAP Risk and Assurance Management enables organizations to continuously oversee risks, detect process deficiencies and compliance issues instantly, and act where it matters most.

As for cockpits in fighter jets, in both cases success depends on integrating critical information into a clear, real-time, and actionable control centre.

What about you, how does your organization design its compliance cockpit? I look forward to reading your thoughts and comments on this blog.

And if you are interested in learning more about SAP solutions for Governance, Risk, and Compliance, feel free to fill-in the demo request form!

entry.title: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 17 - Schema evolution testing
entry.content:
When thinking about schema evolution and starting to plan changes while still in development, the "drop-create" strategy is common.

The cds deploy command will generate the appropriate SQL/DDL statements to effect schema changes. Most commonly this command is run in the planning stages with an option to allow the to-be-executed SQL/DDL statements to be checked, but not executed.

What is that option?

This is a question from the June Developer Challenge on CAP Knowledge. And don't forget: always submit your answer as a hash, on its own - read the Taking part section of the intro post for more info. At the end of today, this question will be updated with links to further reading on this topic.

entry.title: How to Configure OAuth 2.0 Authentication Between SAP Integration Suite (CPI) & S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

I am working on an integration scenario where SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration/CPI) needs to consume APIs from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using OAuth 2.0 authentication.

Scenario

My requirement is to retrieve Sales Order data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition into SAP CPI through an iFlow. Instead of using Basic Authentication, I would like to use OAuth 2.0 for secure communication between CPI and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

What configurations are required in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Integration Suite?

I am looking for guidance on the complete setup required on both SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Integration Suite to enable CPI to securely consume Sales Order data using OAuth 2.0 authentication.

Any documentation references, implementation experiences, or configuration examples would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Confirmation control
entry.content:
Dear @Zlizardo,

Within the BAdI below, only value of limited standard fields and custom fields can be updated.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/8676e556cca85267e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

Modify Purchase Order Item (MM_PUR_S4_PO_MODIFY_ITEM)

With this BAdI, you can

  • Set or change values of custom fields in purchase orders on item level and make changes to the following fields:

    TAXCODE, TAXCOUNTRY, INCOTERMSCLASSIFICATION, INCOTERMSTRANSFERLOCATION, INCOTERMSLOCATION1, INCOTERMSLOCATION2, SHIPPINGTYPE, PURCHASINGINFORECORDUPDATECODE

    Note

    PURCHASINGINFORECORDUPDATECODE is an optional parameter and the available values are:

    Fixed

    Short Description

     

    No update

    A

    Update with or without plant

    B

    Update with plant (if no plant ban)

    C

    Update without plant (if no plant requirement)

  • Use the optional importing parameter: PURCHASEORDERITEM_OLD. This parameter contains the item data of the previous roundtrip, for example, the data before the last change was done.

  • Raise messages by filling the changing parameter table MESSAGES

The mentioned BAdI BADI MM_PUR_S4_PO_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE is used to set the property of these mentioned fields. It is not used to set the default value for fields.

entry.title: Re: Settlement error while settling the project to asset
entry.content:
Hello,

To fix this error, ensure the Profit Center on the WBS element matches the Profit Center on the Asset under Construction (AuC).

Find the mismatch: Check the Profit Center assigned to your WBS element. Then, open the Manage Fixed Assets app, find the AuC, and check its Time-Dependent Data tab to see its Profit Center.

Align the master data: In Manage Fixed Assets, edit the AuC. Update its Cost Center or Profit Center to match the WBS element. If the field is locked, create a New Time Interval valid from the start of the current period.

Best Regards,
Owen
